ocr.text: The Trematominae subfamily, particularly represented by the species T. eulepidotus (Teu), has garnered significant interest in
ichthyological studies due to its unique adaptations to extreme environments in the Antarctic region. The data presented in Table
1 provides a comprehensive overview of key genetic and cytogenetic characteristics of various sub-families within this group,
focusing on chromosomal numbers and C-values. The metric "Chromosomal numbers (2n)" indicates that T. eulepidotus
possesses a diploid chromosomal count of 24, which is consistent with previous findings in related species. Furthermore, the
"C-values (pg) ± SE" for T. eulepidotus is recorded at 1.26 pg, showcasing the genomic size that may influence developmental
and evolutionary traits within this ecological niche.
Sub-families  Genus. species (abbreviation)  Chromosomal numbers (2n)  No. examined  C-values (pg) ± SE  Prior published results (ref)
The "No. examined" column reflects the rigorous sampling methods employed, ensuring that the data is representative of the
population. This meticulous approach enhances the reliability of the results and facilitates comparisons with prior published
results, as indicated in the "Prior published results (ref)" column. Overall, these metrics contribute to a deeper understanding of
the genetic diversity and evolutionary adaptations of Trematominae in response to their challenging habitats.
